name: Holiday Baked Oysters
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Firm white bread (Italian or French or sourdough)
recipeInstructions:
This dish can be made with oysters of any size. While fresh ones are best, thawed, frozen oysters may be used. If desired, prepare the dish and refrigerate it for several hours before baking. Alternatively, you can cut and brown the bread cubes ahead of time. The rest can be finished in minutes once the bread is browned.
1. Remove the crusts from the bread.
2. Cut the bread into 1/4-inch cubes.
3. In a large skillet, melt half the butter.
4. Add 2 cups of bread cubes and toss over LOW HEAT until they are golden-brown.
5. Sprinkle the cubes lightly with salt and cayenne, stirring constantly.
6. Brown the remaining cubes in the remaining butter.
7. Sprinkle again with cayenne and salt.
8. Cover the bottom of a 2-quart baking dish with 1/3 of the bread cubes.
9. Top the bread evenly with half the oysters, sprinkle with salt and cayenne, half the lemon juice, and half the onion.
10. Cover with 1/3 of the bread cubes and sprinkle with salt, cayenne, the rest of the lemon juice, and the onion.
11. Pour in the cream.
12. Top with the last third of the bread cubes.
13. Sprinkle with paprika to decorate.
14. Bake at 325°F for 25 to 35 minutes and serve immediately.
